questions.q: Which of the following is needed to be a "ZigBee Compliant Product"
questions.exp: A ZigBee Compliant Product requires the ZigBee Stack, an Application Profile, and the IEEE 802.15.4 radio standard. IEEE 802.11 is Wi-Fi and is not part of ZigBee.

questions.q: What is the role of Zigbee routers in the Zigbee network?
opts: Acts as a relay between other nodes
questions.exp: ZigBee routers act as relays, forwarding messages between nodes to extend the network range. They do not control the network — that is the coordinator's role.

questions.q: What is the role of Zigbee coordinator in the Zigbee network?
questions.exp: The ZigBee coordinator both controls the network (starts it, manages addresses) and communicates with other nodes — it performs both roles.

questions.q: What is the role of Zigbee end devices in the Zigbee network?
questions.exp: ZigBee end devices only communicate with other nodes (via their parent router or coordinator). They cannot route traffic and do not control the network.

questions.q: Which if the following do not contribute in routing in a ZigBee network?
questions.exp: ZigBee End Devices do not participate in routing — they only send and receive their own data. Routers and the Coordinator both forward packets through the network.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Zigbee communication?
questions.exp: ZigBee is used across Industrial Control, Home Automation, and Healthcare — making 'All of the above' the correct answer.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a feature of Zigbee? (choose the best one)
questions.exp: ZigBee is a low-bandwidth protocol designed for low-power, short-range IoT communication. Large Bandwidth is not a feature — it is intentionally limited to conserve power.

questions.q: Which network topology is not supported by ZigBee?
questions.exp: ZigBee supports Star, Cluster Tree, and Mesh topologies. Bus topology is not supported by ZigBee.
False — ZigBee does not have a transport layer. Its stack consists of the Physical and MAC layers (IEEE 802.15.4), plus the ZigBee Network and Application layers.

questions.q: Which frequency does Z-Wave operate on?
questions.exp: Z-Wave primarily operates at 868 MHz (Europe). It uses sub-GHz frequencies to avoid interference with Wi-Fi and ZigBee which operate at 2.4 GHz.

questions.q: Z-Wave is an implementation of a ZigBee protocol.
questions.exp: False — Z-Wave and ZigBee are completely separate, independent wireless protocols. Z-Wave is developed by Silicon Labs and uses a different frequency and stack from ZigBee.
